"The maximum number of family-sponsored and employment-based preference visas that can be issued to citizens of any country in a fiscal year. The limits are calculated each fiscal year depending on the total number of family-sponsored and employment-based visas available. No more than 7 percent of the visas may be issued to natives of any one independent country in a fiscal year; no more than 2 percent may issued to any one dependency of any independent country." - Definitive match with paraphrasing.

"The per-country limit does not indicate, however, that a country is entitled to the maximum number of visas each year, just that it cannot receive more than that number. Because of the combined workings of the preference system and per-country limits, most countries do not reach this level of visa issuance." - The source explicitly defines 'per-country limit' in the context of family-sponsored and employment-based preference visas, confirming the Wikipedia claim.

"The maximum number of family-sponsored and employment-based preference visas that can be issued to citizens of any country in a fiscal year." - The source text explicitly defines the Per Country Limit as applying to both family-sponsored and employment-based preference visas, directly supporting the claim.

"the nation’s immigrant population grew to 45.4 million in September 2021, a 1.6 million increase since September 2020. The decline almost certainly reflects the significant reduction in new legal and illegal immigration due to Covid-19 restrictions on international travel, the suspension, for a time, of visa processing at American consulates overseas, and Title 42 expulsions, which allowed the U.S. government to send illegal immigrants apprehended at the border immediately back to Mexico, even if the immigrant was not from that country. All of these factors seem to have significantly slowed the pace of new arrivals into the country. At the same time, some level of outmigration continued during this time period as did natural mortality, causing the total immigrant population to fall through the middle of 2020." - The source supports the claim with definitive statements.

"The forces driving ordinary people to leave their homes and put their lives at risk crossing deserts with smugglers to get to the US border are deeply rooted in Central America’s history of inequality and violence, in which the US has long played a defining role." - The source directly supports the claim that poverty and crime are causes of migration.

The source discusses poverty and violence as significant drivers of migration from Central America, and notes US involvement in exacerbating these conditions. However, it does not explicitly state that 'poverty' and 'crime' are causes of migration in a direct, definitive way. It implies these factors but does not enumerate them as explicit causes in a factual statement.

"During the seventeenth century, emergent societies of the English Atlantic were transformed by large-scale migrations of hundreds of thousands of white settlers. Most ended up in colonies that produced the major staples of colonial trade, tobacco and sugar: approximately 210,000 went to the Caribbean, 130,000 to the Chesapeake (Virginia and Maryland), 24,000 to the Middle Colonies, and 21,000 to New England." The source provides a total of approximately 385,000 English migrants to the specified American colonies (210,000 + 130,000 + 24,000 + 21,000). This closely supports the claim of 'approximately 400,000 English people migrated to America' under European colonization in the 17th century.

The source states that Saudi-backed forces moved on Aden and that government forces seized control of the international airport and the presidential palace. "Saudi-backed ground forces on Wednesday moved on the Yemeni city of Aden, a stronghold of the southern secessionists supported by the UAE. ... forces loyal to the separatist Southern Transitional Council (STC) pulled out from all districts of Aden governorate, and its troops seized control of the international airport. ... some officials from the STC, however, said the group is still in control of Aden. ... STC deputy leader ... announced a curfew ... STC said al-Zubaidi is in Aden and 'continues his duties'". The source supports that Saudi-backed forces moved to capture Aden and that the STC leader vanished, but it does not definitively state that Aden was captured or that the STC dissolved. There is conflicting information about control of Aden.

The source partially supports the claim. However, it does not state that the STC was dissolved or that its forces collapsed; in fact, it quotes STC officials claiming they still control Aden and that al-Zubaidi is still in Aden performing his duties. The claim about dissolution and collapse is not supported by the source, which presents a contested situation.

В тексте прямо указывается, что власти отгородились от населения, милиция не разрешала митинги, что привело к насильственному ворванию в здания партии и других органов, требованиям высылки чеченцев и других деталей, полностью соответствующих формулировке вики-статьи.
